title: Eric Weinstein on the deep state surveillance tactics.
summary: Eric Weinstein discusses NSA surveillance capabilities and the paradox of mass data collection - while intelligence agencies can monitor everything, they rarely act on this information because doing so would reveal the extent of their surveillance programs.

acronyms.definition: U.S. intelligence agency responsible for signals intelligence and cybersecurity

key_claims.claim: The NSA has a back door into everything and can hear and listen to everything people do and say, which is indexed and searchable
Most information collected by surveillance agencies is effectively useless because acting on it would reveal the extent of their surveillance capabilities
The surveillance system only works if those conducting surveillance rarely intrude into people's lives in identifiable ways
